label: Major banks set to receive first stablecoin issuance licenses in Hong Kong
label_zh: 主要銀行將獲發香港首批穩定幣發行牌照

timeline.title: HSBC and Standard Chartered Expected to Secure Hong Kong’s First Regulated Stablecoin Issuance Licenses
timeline.title_zh: 滙豐和渣打預計將獲得香港首批受規管的穩定幣發行牌照
timeline.sub_title: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority is reportedly prioritizing note-issuing banks to spearhead its new regulatory framework, aiming to establish a high-compliance foundation for the city’s digital asset ecosystem.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 香港金融管理局據報將優先考慮發鈔銀行，以推動其新的監管框架，旨在為本港的數碼資產生態系統建立高合規的基礎。
timeline.summary:
Hong Kong is positioned to reach a defining milestone in its digital asset evolution as reports emerge that HSBC and a joint venture led by Standard Chartered are the frontrunners to receive the city's first stablecoin issuer licenses. According to sources familiar with the matter, the Hong Kong Monetary Authority is preparing to grant these landmark approvals within the next two weeks, with a potential announcement date targeted for March 24. This [ref:9, 7] move follows a rigorous assessment of dozens of applications submitted under the territory’s new Stablecoins Ordinance, which officially took effect last August.

The anticipated selection of these specific institutions reflects a deliberate regulatory strategy to prioritize established, note-issuing banks. By placing [ref:7, 3, 1] well-capitalized incumbents at the center of the stablecoin market, regulators aim to leverage existing compliance frameworks and strong capital reserves to ensure financial stability. This "stricter [ref:5, 10] before looser" approach is designed to foster institutional and retail trust in tokenized payments, contrasting with the more volatile nature of the broader cryptocurrency market. Financial Secretary Paul Chan previously indicated that only a small number of licenses—likely three or four—would be issued in this inaugural batch to maintain tight oversight during the early stages of adoption.

Standard Chartered is expected to receive its license through a joint venture formed with Animoca Brands and Hong Kong Telecommunications. This entity has been a prominent participant in the HKMA’s stablecoin issuer sandbox since 2024, testing use cases such as e-commerce payments and cross-border trade settlements. In contrast, the potential [ref:7] approval of HSBC has come as a surprise to some industry observers, as the bank did not participate in the official sandbox program. However, its status [ref:7] as Hong Kong’s largest bank and its extensive experience with tokenized deposits and blockchain-based trade finance reportedly strengthened its application in the eyes of the regulator.

The licensing regime requires all fiat-referenced stablecoin issuers to meet stringent requirements, including 100% reserve backing in high-quality liquid assets and the ability to offer par redemptions within one business day. These standards are nearly identical to traditional banking regulations, signaling Hong Kong’s intent to integrate blockchain technology into its core financial infrastructure. While the HKMA has declined [ref:4] to comment on specific market rumors, the impending approvals are viewed as a critical step in the city’s bid to become a global hub for digital assets and tokenization. This progress arrives as neighboring mainland China continues to maintain a strict stance against onshore real-world asset tokenization, further widening the regulatory gap and positioning Hong Kong as a unique gateway for compliant digital finance in Asia.
timeline.summary_zh:
隨著消息傳出，滙豐銀行及由渣打銀行牽頭的合資企業將領取本港首批穩定幣發行牌照，香港有望在其數碼資產演進中達到一個決定性的里程碑。據知情人士透露，香港金融管理局正準備在未來兩週內授予這些標誌性的批准，預計宣布日期為 3 月 24 日。此舉是在對該地區新《穩定幣條例》（去年 8 月正式生效）進行了嚴格評估後作出的，該條例收到了數十份申請。[ref:9, 7]

這些特定機構的預計選擇反映了一項審慎的監管策略，即優先考慮成熟、發鈔的銀行。透過將資本雄厚的現有銀行置於穩定幣市場的中心，監管機構旨在利用現有的合規框架和雄厚的資本儲備來確保金融穩定。這種「先嚴後寬」的做法旨在培養機構和零售客戶對代幣化支付的信任，與更廣泛的加密貨幣市場的波動性形成對比。財政司司長陳茂波先前曾指出，在首批發放的牌照中，可能只會發放三到四個牌照，以在早期採用階段保持嚴格的監管。[ref:7, 3, 1, 5, 10]

渣打銀行預計將透過與 Animoca Brands 和香港電訊組成的合資企業獲得其牌照。該實體自 2024 年以來一直是香港金管局穩定幣發行沙盒計劃的活躍參與者，測試了電子商務支付和跨境貿易結算等用例。相比之下，滙豐銀行的潛在批准讓一些業內觀察人士感到意外，因為該銀行沒有參加官方沙盒計劃。然而，據稱其作為香港最大銀行的地位以及在代幣化存款和基於區塊鏈的貿易融資方面的豐富經驗，在監管機構眼中加強了其申請。[ref:7]

牌照制度要求所有法幣掛鈎的穩定幣發行商滿足嚴格的要求，包括持有高質量流動資產的 100% 儲備支持，並能在一個工作日內以面值贖回。這些標準與傳統銀行監管幾乎相同，表明香港有意將區塊鏈技術整合到其核心金融基礎設施中。雖然金管局拒絕評論具體市場傳言，但即將到來的批准被視為該市爭取成為數碼資產和代幣化全球中心的關鍵一步。這一進展發生之際，鄰近的中國內地繼續對境內真實資產代幣化採取嚴格立場，進一步擴大了監管差距，並將香港定位為亞洲合規數碼金融的獨特門戶。[ref:4]

timeline.title: Major Banks HSBC and Standard Chartered Poised to Secure Hong Kong’s First Regulated Stablecoin Licenses
timeline.title_zh: 主要銀行匯豐及渣打銀行有望獲得香港首批受規管穩定幣牌照
timeline.sub_title: Following a rigorous review of dozens of applicants, Hong Kong regulators are reportedly preparing to issue inaugural licenses to prominent financial institutions, signaling a shift toward institutional digital assets.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 在嚴格審查數十家申請者後，香港監管機構據報正準備向知名金融機構發放首批牌照，標誌著機構數字資產的轉向。
timeline.summary:
Hong Kong is on the verge of a significant milestone in its digital asset journey, with reports emerging on March 12, 2026, that the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) is prepared to grant its first batch of stablecoin issuance licenses. Sources familiar with the matter indicate that the inaugural approvals are expected to be announced as early as next week, with March 24 cited as a potential target date. The [ref:6] shortlist for this landmark regulatory move reportedly features global banking giants HSBC and Standard Chartered, alongside the Hong Kong-based virtual asset trading platform OSL. This [ref:4] development follows a period of intense scrutiny by the HKMA, which narrowed down a field of approximately 36 formal applications to a select group of institutional frontrunners.

The inclusion of HSBC is particularly notable among industry analysts. Unlike many other applicants, HSBC did not participate in the HKMA’s stablecoin issuer sandbox launched last year. However, the bank has been deeply integrated into the city's broader tokenization ecosystem, participating in "Project Ensemble"—a wholesale central bank digital currency (CBDC) pilot—and launching retail-facing products like the HSBC Gold Token. Standard Chartered [ref:7], by contrast, has pursued the license through a high-profile joint venture involving Animoca Brands and HKT, having been an active participant in the regulatory sandbox. The selection of these established financial institutions underscores the HKMA’s "stricter before looser" philosophy, prioritizing entities with robust compliance frameworks and significant capital reserves to ensure market stability.

Under the regulatory framework established by the Stablecoin Ordinance that took effect in August 2025, licensed issuers must adhere to stringent bank-grade requirements. These include maintaining a minimum paid-up share capital of HK$25 million and ensuring that all stablecoins are 100% backed by high-quality liquid assets held in trust. Furthermore, issuers [ref:2] must guarantee the redemption of tokens at par value within one business day. By focusing the first [ref:2] batch on note-issuing banks and regulated platforms, Hong Kong aims to mitigate the risks of "digital dollarization"—the potential for the digital economy to be dominated by unregulated, foreign-denominated tokens—while promoting a regulated, Hong Kong dollar-pegged alternative for local and cross-border trade.

While the market originally expected a slightly larger initial cohort, recent reports suggest the HKMA has opted for a more cautious rollout. Some previously discussed [ref:7, 3] candidates, including RD Technologies and certain mainland Chinese payment providers, were reportedly excluded from this first round. This selectivity is seen as [ref:1] a strategic move to establish a credible and secure foundation for the city's digital currency architecture. Financial Secretary Paul Chan had previously hinted at this timeline during his 2026-27 budget speech, emphasizing that the government would facilitate licensed issuers to explore various application scenarios, such as real-time corporate treasury management and the settlement of tokenized bonds. As the March 24 window approaches, the financial sector anticipates that these licenses will finally bridge the gap between traditional banking infrastructure and the programmable future of decentralized finance in Asia.
timeline.summary_zh:
根據3月12日傳出的消息，香港即將迎來數字資產領域的一個重要里程碑，香港金融管理局（HKMA）已準備好頒發其首批穩定幣發行牌照。知情人士透露，首批批准預計最早下周公布，並可能在3月24日。據報道，入圍這項里程碑式監管行動名單的包括全球銀行巨頭匯豐銀行（HSBC）和渣打銀行（Standard Chartered），以及香港虛擬資產交易平台OSL。此舉是香港金管局經過對約36份正式申請進行嚴格篩選後，選定了一批機構領跑者的結果。[ref:6, 4]

匯豐銀行在此次入圍名單中尤為引人注目。與許多其他申請者不同，匯豐並未參與金管局去年啟動的穩定幣發行人監管沙盒。然而，該銀行已深度融入本港的代幣化生態系統，參與了批發央行數字貨幣（CBDC）試點「Project Ensemble」，並推出了如匯豐黃金代幣等零售產品。相比之下，渣打銀行通過與Animoca Brands和HKT組成的知名合資企業申請牌照，並且一直是監管沙盒的積極參與者。這些知名金融機構的入選，凸顯了金管局「審慎監管、循序漸進」的策略，優先考慮擁有健全合規框架和充足資本儲備的機構，以確保市場穩定。[ref:7]

根據2025年8月生效的《穩定幣條例》設立的監管框架，持牌發行人必須遵守嚴格的銀行級要求。這包括維持最低實繳股本2500萬港元，並確保所有穩定幣100%由信託持有的高質量流動資產支持。此外，發行人必須保證代幣按面值在一個工作日內兌換。通過將首批牌照集中發放給發鈔銀行和受監管平台，香港旨在減輕「數字美元化」的風險，即數字經濟可能被未經監管的、以美元計價的代幣主導，同時推廣一種受監管、與港元掛鉤的替代方案，用於本地和跨境貿易。[ref:2]

儘管市場最初預期會有更多機構獲得牌照，但最新報道顯示，香港金管局選擇了更為謹慎的發牌方式。據報，RD Technologies和一些中國內地支付供應商等先前討論過的候選機構，未能進入首輪。這種選擇性被視為建立香港數字貨幣架構可信且安全基礎的戰略舉措。財政司司長陳茂波在2026-27年度財政預算案演講中曾暗示過這一時間表，強調政府將協助持牌發行人探索各種應用場景，如實時企業財資管理和代幣化債券的結算。隨著3月24日的臨近，金融界預計這些牌照將最終彌合傳統銀行基礎設施與亞洲去中心化金融可編程未來之間的差距。[ref:7, 3, 1]
